messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: Having OTA is great - just did an upgrade on my remote H4 via WiFi. But what about nRF repeaters with only BLE? Possible to OTA via BLE for these and hope the ones up on the tower have a strong enough BLE signal to send the FW file standing below? Otherwise it's a royal pain to climb up and retrieve then bring down to update then back up to install again. (edited)
messages.content_html: Having OTA is great - just did an upgrade on my remote H4 via WiFi. But what about nRF repeaters with only BLE? Possible to OTA via BLE for these and hope the ones up on the tower have a strong enough BLE signal to send the FW file standing below? Otherwise it's a royal pain to climb up and retrieve then bring down to update then back up to install again. (edited)

messages.author: 🦦 Hilton Travis SMBI
messages.content_html: Yes (edited)

messages.author: skykingisepic
messages.text: Any info on how to OTA using BLE? I have the SenseCAP Solar in OTA mode and no clue how to upload a FW file. Search found nothing on the process. BT on phone sees SENSECAP_SOLAR_OTA but won't Connect. (edited)
messages.content_html: Any info on how to OTA using BLE? I have the SenseCAP Solar in OTA mode and no clue how to upload a FW file. Search found nothing on the process. BT on phone sees SENSECAP_SOLAR_OTA but won't Connect. (edited)

messages.author: 🦦 Hilton Travis SMBI
messages.text: Log into remote management over LoRa, enter "start ota" then use the nrf DFU app on your phone to update the OS. But please be sure you have the otafix bootcode update to drastically increase both speed and chance of success. (edited)
messages.content_html: Log into remote management over LoRa, enter "start ota" then use the nrf DFU app on your phone to update the OS. But please be sure you have the otafix bootcode update to drastically increase both speed and chance of success. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Submarine_Man
messages.text: This is using an Apple iPhone This supports most but not all of what is available on the Web Flasher 1) Start the MeshCore App. Connect to your repeater. 2) Click on your repeater in the Contact list, enter the admin Password 3) Click on Command Line 4) enter: start ota (Must be all lower case or you will get Unknown Command 5) Use “MToolsBLE RFID Reader”that is available on the Apple Store. 6) Click on the device / MeshCore big button 7) Click MeshCore 8) Select: Type: Repeater Firmware Device: what your repeater is based on (mine RAK4631) It will download the firmware and say Ready under File 9) Click on Device “Choose” It will show you the BLE devices that are within range. Select the OTA one 10) Click “Start” It will go faster with a stronger BLE signal (I reached 1.5k baud and it took 5 minutes (edited)
messages.content_html: This is using an Apple iPhone This supports most but not all of what is available on the Web Flasher 1) Start the MeshCore App. Connect to your repeater. 2) Click on your repeater in the Contact list, enter the admin Password 3) Click on Command Line 4) enter: start ota (Must be all lower case or you will get Unknown Command 5) Use “MToolsBLE RFID Reader”that is available on the Apple Store. 6) Click on the device / MeshCore big button 7) Click MeshCore 8) Select: Type: Repeater Firmware Device: what your repeater is based on (mine RAK4631) It will download the firmware and say Ready under File 9) Click on Device “Choose” It will show you the BLE devices that are within range. Select the OTA one 10) Click “Start” It will go faster with a stronger BLE signal (I reached 1.5k baud and it took 5 minutes (edited)
